ucs.title: [LLM] BRONZE BUTLER (Tick) — Daserf/xxmm DLL side-loading via signed-binary proxy in user-writable paths
ucs.description: Hunts BRONZE BUTLER's signature loader chain: a legitimately signed third-party binary (commonly a Symantec, Trend Micro, Hewlett-Packard, or Kaspersky utility) is dropped into AppData/ProgramData/Public/Temp and used to sideload a co-resident malicious DLL (Daserf, xxmm/Minzen, Datper). This T1574.001 + T1036.005 + T1027 chain is one of Tick's most consistent fingerprints across a decade of intrusions in Japan.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as command_line values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_hash) as proc_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_path IN ("*\\AppData\\Local\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\Downloads\\*") Processes.process_name IN ("rcsync.exe","vncviewer.exe","GUP.exe","avp.exe","avpui.exe","DefenderUI.exe","PresentationHost.exe","hpqtra08.exe","hkcmd.exe","smax4pnp.exe","NMSAccessU.exe","OneDrive.exe","ccSvcHst.exe","SMC.exe","NavLogon.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id _time
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| eval proc_dir=replace(process_path,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$","")
| join type=inner dest proc_dir [
    | tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count values(Filesystem.file_name) as dropped_dll values(Filesystem.process_name) as dropper values(Filesystem.file_hash) as dll_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`
    | eval proc_dir=replace(file_path,"\\\\[^\\\\]+$","")
    | fields dest proc_dir dropped_dll dropper dll_hash
  ]
| where dropper!=process_name
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path proc_hash command_line dropped_dll dll_hash dropper parent
ucs.kql:
// BRONZE BUTLER / Tick — signed legit binary side-loading from user-writable path
let _suspicious_paths = dynamic([@"\\AppData\\", @"\\ProgramData\\", @"\\Users\\Public\\", @"\\Windows\\Temp\\", @"\\Downloads\\"]);
let _vendor_decoys = dynamic(["Symantec","Trend Micro","Kaspersky","Hewlett-Packard","HP Inc.","Hewlett Packard","Sourcenext","Lhaplus","NEC","Fujitsu","Justsystems","Sky Co.,LTD."]);
let SignedBinaryRunFromUserPath =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_vendor_decoys)
         or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_vendor_decoys)
    | where FolderPath has_any (_suspicious_paths)
    | where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files") and not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)")
    | project ProcTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ProcCompany = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              ParentCompany = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName;
// Side-loaded DLL = different SHA256, same folder, loaded by the signed proxy
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (_suspicious_paths)
| where FolderPath =~ InitiatingProcessFolderPath           // co-resident DLL drop
| where SHA256 != InitiatingProcessSHA256
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (_vendor_decoys)
| join kind=inner SignedBinaryRunFromUserPath on $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessSHA256 == $right.SHA256
| where Timestamp between (ProcTime .. ProcTime + 5m)
| project LoadTime = Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ProxyBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ProxyVendor = InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
          ProxyPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ProxySHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          SideLoadedDLL = FileName,
          DLLPath = FolderPath,
          DLLSHA256 = SHA256,
          ParentImage, ParentCmd
| order by LoadTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BRONZE BUTLER / Tick — signed legit binary running from a user-writable path is the
// process-create half of the Daserf side-load chain. Sentinel SecurityEvent does not carry
// DLL ImageLoad telemetry natively, so we approximate via 4688 + ImFileEvent join.
let _suspicious_paths = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Downloads\"]);
let _vendor_proxy_names = dynamic(["rcsync.exe","vncviewer.exe","GUP.exe","avp.exe","avpui.exe","hpqtra08.exe","NMSAccessU.exe","ccSvcHst.exe","SMC.exe","NavLogon.exe","hkcmd.exe","PresentationHost.exe"]);
let ProxyExec =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
    | where BinName in~ (_vendor_proxy_names)
    | where NewProcessName has_any (_suspicious_paths)
    | extend ProxyDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(NewProcessName,"\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
    | project ProxyTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, BinName, NewProcessName, ProxyDir, ParentProcessName;
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
| where TargetFilePath has_any (_suspicious_paths)
| extend DropDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(TargetFilePath,"\\"), 0, -2), "\\")
| join kind=inner ProxyExec on $left.DvcHostname == $right.Computer, $left.DropDir == $right.ProxyDir
| where TimeGenerated between (ProxyTime - 1h .. ProxyTime + 5m)
| project DropTime = TimeGenerated, ProxyTime, Computer, Account,
          ProxyBinary = BinName, ProxyPath = NewProcessName,
          SideLoadedDLL = TargetFileName, DLLPath = TargetFilePath, DLLSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256,
          ParentProcessName
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.rationale: Tick / BRONZE BUTLER (G0060) has used the same loader recipe across Daserf, xxmm/Minzen, Datper and Ghostdown campaigns — drop a legitimately signed Japanese-market or AV vendor binary alongside a malicious DLL into a user-writable directory and let DLL search-order hijacking run the implant under a trusted parent. Trend Micro (Nov 2017) and Secureworks (Oct 2017) both fingerprint this chain. The

ucs.title: [LLM] BRONZE BUTLER (Tick) — Office/archive-spawned 'at.exe' or schtasks persistence (Daserf installer pattern)
ucs.description: Catches Tick's documented post-exploitation step: after a spearphishing LZH/RAR/maldoc payload executes, the unpacked dropper registers persistence with the legacy 'at.exe' command or schtasks /create — frequently invoking a binary in AppData/Public/Temp. The Office/archive-handler parent + at.exe child combination is highly characteristic of Tick installers (T1566.001 → T1204.002 → T1053.002/T1053.005).
ucs.splunk:
| tstats `security_content_summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("at.exe","schtasks.exe") (Processes.process="*create*" OR Processes.process="* /sc *" OR Processes.process="* /ru *" OR Processes.process="* /tr *") Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","explorer.exe","WinRAR.exe","Lhaplus.exe","7zFM.exe","7zG.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where match(cmdline,"(?i)\\\\AppData\\\\|\\\\ProgramData\\\\|\\\\Users\\\\Public\\\\|\\\\Temp\\\\|\\\\Downloads\\\\")
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| table firstTime lastTime dest user parent_process_name parent_cmdline process_name cmdline path
ucs.kql:
// BRONZE BUTLER — at.exe / schtasks persistence chained from Office or archive-handler parent
let _office_archive_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","explorer.exe","winrar.exe","lhaplus.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","hh.exe"]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\\AppData\\", @"\\ProgramData\\", @"\\Users\\Public\\", @"\\Windows\\Temp\\", @"\\Downloads\\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ ("at.exe","schtasks.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_office_archive_parents)
   or InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ (_office_archive_parents)
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/create","/sc ","/tr ","/ru ") or FileName =~ "at.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_user_writable)
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\(AppData|ProgramData|Public|Temp|Downloads)\\"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Tool = FileName,
          TaskCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          IsRemote = AdditionalFields
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BRONZE BUTLER — at.exe / schtasks persistence chained from Office or archive parent
let _office_archive_parents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","explorer.exe","winrar.exe","lhaplus.exe","7zfm.exe","7zg.exe","hh.exe"]);
let _user_writable = dynamic([@"\AppData\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\Windows\Temp\", @"\Downloads\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ParentBin = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in ("at.exe","schtasks.exe")
| where ParentBin in (_office_archive_parents)
| where CommandLine has_any ("/create","/sc ","/tr ","/ru ") or ChildBin == "at.exe"
| where CommandLine has_any (_user_writable)
   or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\(AppData|ProgramData|Public|Temp|Downloads)\\"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Tool = ChildBin, TaskCmd = CommandLine,
          ParentImage = ParentBin, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BRONZE BUTLER Persistence via at.exe or schtasks Spawned by Office or Archive Handler
id: 5b9c2d3e-7c1b-4d8e-9c10-tick-at-schtasks
status: experimental
description: Detects Tick / BRONZE BUTLER post-exploitation persistence where at.exe or schtasks.exe is invoked by an Office product or Japanese archive handler (Lhaplus / WinRAR / 7-Zip) and registers a task pointing at a user-writable path.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0060/
  - https://www.secureworks.com/research/bronze-butler-targets-japanese-businesses
  - https://documents.trendmicro.com/assets/Tech-Brief-Untangling-the-Patchwork-Cyberespionage-Group.pdf
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1053.002
  - attack.t1053.005
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.g0060
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\at.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\excel.exe'
      - '\powerpnt.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\winrar.exe'
      - '\Lhaplus.exe'
      - '\7zFM.exe'
      - '\7zG.exe'
      - '\hh.exe'
  selection_writable:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '\AppData\'
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '\Downloads\'
  condition: selection_child and selection_parent and selection_writable
fields:
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - ParentCommandLine
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IT scripts deploying tasks via Office macros (rare; allowlist by signed publisher)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Tick has historically reached for the legacy 'at' command — unusual on modern Windows and almost never invoked by Office or archive handlers in legitimate operations — alongside schtasks /create to anchor Daserf and follow-on tools. The parent set explicitly includes Lhaplus, the Japanese-market archiver Tick weaponises in spearphishing LZH bundles per Trend Micro 2017/2019 reporting. This combina
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
